name: 21_Iris_Classification_SVM_Iris_ML
query: I request a project to classify iris species utilizing the Iris dataset with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier implemented in `src/model.py`. The project should standardize the data in and perform feature selection in `src/data_loader.py`. It will document the classification accuracy and save it as `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt`, and generate and save a confusion matrix as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. It will further create an interactive web application in `src/app.py` using Streamlit to showcase classification results and model performance, with the figures stored in `results/figures/`. The web page should be user-friendly, with a brief explanation of the model to help users understand how the SVM classifier works.

requirements.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.
Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.
A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

preferences.criteria: The Streamlit web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily explore different aspects of the model's performance.
A brief model explanation should be included on the web page, helping users understand how the SVM classifier works.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> No evidence shows the Iris dataset is actually used. In `src/data_loader.py`, `load_dataset()` only returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and does not import or load `sklearn.datasets.load_iris` or any Iris data file. The provided trajectory evidence also explicitly says there are no recorded executions or logs confirming use of the Iris dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: Data is standardized to ensure feature values are within the same range in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, there is no actual standardization logic applied to data. The file only includes a placeholder `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function returning a list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`, but these are not connected to any dataset processing. No scaler (e.g., `StandardScaler`) or feature-value normalization code is implemented.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature selection is performed to identify important features in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` only mentions the requirement text in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and returns placeholder data in `load_dataset()`. There is no actual feature-selection logic, no import or use of selection methods (e.g., `SelectKBest`, RFE, importance scores), and no output identifying important features. The `build_transforms()` function lists placeholder transforms, which is unrelated to feature selection.

<UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement an SVM classifier. The file only contains benchmark text metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns the string `"model placeholder"`; there is no SVM class/import (e.g., `sklearn.svm.SVC`) or training/model logic present.

<UNSATISFIED> `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than an actual saved classification accuracy value or metric. This indicates the required classification accuracy was not properly written to the file.

judge_stats.criteria: A confusion matrix is generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The required file path `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` exists in the workspace tree, but the provided file evidence shows it is not a valid readable image: `"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`. Additionally, no trajectory or execution evidence confirms successful generation or saving of a confusion matrix image.

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web application `src/app.py` is created using "Streamlit"` to showcase classification results and model performance in results/figures/.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/app.py` does not implement an interactive Streamlit web app. The file only defines metadata-like constants (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and a `main()` that returns a dictionary; it contains no `import streamlit`, no Streamlit UI calls (e.g. `st.title`, `st.image`), and no code that loads or displays assets from `results/figures/` such as `confusion_matrix.png`. The presence of `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` alone does not show that `app.py` showcases it.
